name: Chandrayaan-3
description: India's third lunar mission achieved a historic soft landing near the Moon's south pole.

objectives.title: Soft landing on the Moon
objectives.info: Demonstrate autonomous precision landing technology near the lunar south pole.

objectives.title: Deploy Pragyan rover
objectives.info: Validate rover deployment and surface mobility on the Moon.

objectives.title: Perform in-situ experiments
objectives.info: Collect scientific measurements directly from the lunar surface.

achievements.title: Historic lunar south pole landing
achievements.info: Became the first nation to achieve a successful soft landing near the Moon's south polar region.

achievements.title: Fourth nation to soft-land on the Moon
achievements.info: India joined the United States, Soviet Union (Russia), and China as the fourth country to successfully soft-land a spacecraft on the Moon.

achievements.title: Successful Pragyan rover deployment
achievements.info: The Pragyan rover rolled out from the Vikram lander and conducted in-situ scientific experiments on the lunar surface.

achievements.title: Validated critical landing technologies
achievements.info: Successfully demonstrated autonomous guidance, navigation, hazard detection, and controlled descent technologies for future lunar missions.

payloads.title: ChaSTE
payloads.info: Measured the thermal properties of lunar soil.

payloads.title: ILSA
payloads.info: Detected seismic activity and vibrations on the Moon.

payloads.title: RAMBHA-LP
payloads.info: Measured plasma density near the lunar surface.

engineering_significance: Validated autonomous precision landing, hazard detection, guidance algorithms, and propulsion technologies required for future lunar and planetary missions.

timeline.event: Launch aboard LVM3 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre
Successfully entered lunar orbit
Historic soft landing near the Moon's south pole
Pragyan rover deployed and began scientific experiments

name: Mangalyaan
description: India became the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit and the first country to succeed on its first attempt.

objectives.title: Demonstrate interplanetary mission capability
objectives.info: Validate India's ability to design, launch and operate a spacecraft on an interplanetary mission.

objectives.title: Study the Martian surface and atmosphere
objectives.info: Collect scientific data on Mars' surface features, atmosphere and mineral composition.

objectives.title: Develop deep-space communication
objectives.info: Test navigation, tracking and communication technologies over interplanetary distances.

achievements.title: First Asian nation to reach Mars
achievements.info: India became the first Asian country to successfully place a spacecraft in Mars orbit.

achievements.title: Succeeded on the first attempt
achievements.info: India became the first nation to reach Mars orbit on its maiden mission.

achievements.title: Highly cost-effective mission
achievements.info: Completed one of the world's lowest-cost interplanetary missions.

achievements.title: Exceeded planned mission life
achievements.info: Operated for several years beyond its original six-month design life.

payloads.title: Mars Colour Camera (MCC)
payloads.info: Captured high-resolution images of Mars and its moons.

payloads.title: Methane Sensor for Mars (MSM)
payloads.info: Searched for traces of methane, a possible indicator of geological or biological activity.

payloads.title: Lyman Alpha Photometer (LAP)
payloads.info: Studied hydrogen and deuterium in the Martian atmosphere.

payloads.title: Thermal Infrared Imaging Spectrometer (TIS)
payloads.info: Mapped the mineral composition and surface temperature of Mars.

payloads.title: MENCA
payloads.info: Measured the composition of the Martian exosphere.

engineering_significance: Demonstrated India's ability to design, navigate, and operate an interplanetary spacecraft while achieving exceptional cost efficiency and reliability.

timeline.event: Launched aboard PSLV-C25
Departed Earth's sphere of influence
Successfully entered Mars orbit
Conducted scientific observations and exceeded planned mission life

name: Aditya-L1
description: India's first dedicated mission to study the Sun.

objectives.title: Study the Sun's corona
objectives.info: Observe the outermost layer of the Sun to understand coronal heating.

objectives.title: Monitor solar activity
objectives.info: Study solar flares, coronal mass ejections and magnetic fields.

objectives.title: Improve space weather prediction
objectives.info: Provide data to better understand solar events affecting Earth and satellites.

achievements.title: India's first solar observatory
achievements.info: The country's first dedicated mission to study the Sun.

achievements.title: Reached the L1 point
achievements.info: Successfully entered a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point.

achievements.title: Continuous solar observation
achievements.info: Provides uninterrupted observations of the Sun without Earth's eclipses.

payloads.title: VELC
payloads.info: Observes the solar corona in visible and infrared wavelengths.

payloads.title: SUIT
payloads.info: Captures ultraviolet images of the Sun's photosphere and chromosphere.

payloads.title: SoLEXS
payloads.info: Measures low-energy solar X-ray emissions.

payloads.title: HEL1OS
payloads.info: Observes high-energy solar X-rays.

payloads.title: ASPEX
payloads.info: Studies particles in the solar wind.

payloads.title: PAPA
payloads.info: Measures the composition of solar wind plasma.

payloads.title: Magnetometer
payloads.info: Measures magnetic fields at the L1 Lagrange point.

engineering_significance: Established India's capability to continuously observe the Sun from the L1 Lagrange point, improving space-weather forecasting and advancing heliophysics research.

timeline.event: Launched aboard PSLV-C57
Performed Trans-Lagrange Injection
Successfully inserted into halo orbit around the L1 point
Continuous observation of the Sun and collection of scientific data

name: Gaganyaan

objectives.title: Demonstrate human spaceflight capability
objectives.info: Show India's ability to independently send astronauts into space.

objectives.title: Develop indigenous crew systems
objectives.info: Build crew modules, life-support systems and recovery technologies within India.

objectives.title: Ensure astronaut safety
objectives.info: Validate emergency escape, re-entry and recovery systems.

achievements.title: Crew Escape Test successful
achievements.info: Validated the emergency escape system through a successful test flight.

achievements.title: Major indigenous technologies developed
achievements.info: Built key human-spaceflight technologies within India.

achievements.title: Foundation for future crewed missions
achievements.info: Establishes capabilities for long-term human space exploration.

payloads.title: Crew Module
payloads.info: Pressurized spacecraft that carries astronauts during the mission.

payloads.title: Service Module
payloads.info: Provides propulsion, electrical power and mission support.

payloads.title: Environmental Control & Life Support System
payloads.info: Maintains breathable air, temperature and pressure for the crew.

payloads.title: Crew Escape System
payloads.info: Rapidly separates the crew module from the rocket during emergencies.

payloads.title: Human-rated LVM3
payloads.info: A modified LVM3 launch vehicle certified for carrying astronauts.

engineering_significance: Developing indigenous human spaceflight technologies—including crew safety, life support, and human-rated launch systems—lays the foundation for India's future crewed space missions.

timeline.event: Crew Escape System Test Vehicle mission successfully completed
Uncrewed orbital test missions and system validation
First crewed Gaganyaan mission to Low Earth Orbit
Recovery of astronauts after a safe splashdown in the Indian Ocean

name: AstroSat
description: India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory designed to study celestial objects across ultraviolet and X-ray wavelengths.
engineering_significance: Established India's capability to build and operate a space observatory with multiple scientific instruments observing the universe simultaneously.

objectives.title: Study compact stars
objectives.info: Observe neutron stars, pulsars and black holes in multiple wavelengths.

objectives.title: Observe high-energy universe
objectives.info: Study energetic cosmic phenomena such as quasars and X-ray binaries.

objectives.title: Multi-wavelength astronomy
objectives.info: Observe celestial objects simultaneously in ultraviolet and X-ray bands.

payloads.title: UVIT
payloads.info: Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope captures high-resolution ultraviolet images.

payloads.title: LAXPC
payloads.info: Large Area X-ray Proportional Counter studies X-ray emissions from compact objects.

payloads.title: CZTI
payloads.info: Cadmium Zinc Telluride Imager observes hard X-rays and gamma-ray bursts.

payloads.title: SXT
payloads.info: Soft X-ray Telescope studies low-energy X-ray sources.

payloads.title: SSM
payloads.info: Scanning Sky Monitor detects transient X-ray events.

achievements.title: India's first space observatory
achievements.info: Marked India's entry into dedicated space-based astronomy.

achievements.title: International scientific collaboration
achievements.info: Used by researchers worldwide for astrophysical observations.

achievements.title: Long operational life
achievements.info: Continues producing valuable scientific data well beyond its planned lifetime.

timeline.event: Successfully launched aboard PSLV-C30.
Scientific instruments commissioned.
Began regular scientific observations.
Continues astronomical observations.
